---
title: "About"
---

![](files/profile.png){width=180px style="border-radius: 50%; float: right; margin-left: 20px;"}

Hello! I am **Francesco D’Amico**, a PhD student in Physics at **Sapienza University of Rome**, where I work under the supervision of Matteo Negri and Chiara Cammarota on the topic of **statistical theory of learning**.  
My research lies at the intersection of **statistical mechanics, machine learning, and complex systems**.   

---

## Research Interests
- **Data structure** in machine learning and **neural scaling laws** 
- **Associative memories** and **attention**-class architectures  
- **Energy-based models** and **approximate likelihood** methods   
- **Optimization and inference** in hard computational tasks  

---

## Academic Background
- **PhD in Physics**, Sapienza University of Rome (2023–2026)  
- **Visiting PhD Student**, École polytechnique fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL) - Matthieu Wyart - PCSL group (March - May 2026)
- **Visiting PhD Student**, Complutense University of Madrid - Group of Beatriz Seoane (March - May 2025) 
- **Master’s Degree in Theoretical Physics**, Sapienza University of Rome, *110/110 cum laude* (2021-2023)  
- **Physics department excellence program**, Sapienza University of Rome (2018-2023) 
- **Bachelor’s Degree in Physics**, Sapienza University of Rome, *110/110 cum laude* (2018-2021)  
 
---

## Contact
- 📧 [francesco.damico@uniroma1.it](mailto:francesco.damico@uniroma1.it)  
- 💻 [GitHub](https://github.com/Francill99)  
- 📑 [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=fcoliR8AAAAJ&hl=it)  

---

## Download CV
- [English CV](docs/CV_english.pdf)  
- [Italian Europass CV](docs/CV_italian.pdf)
